# testdata provenance

These files are **test fixtures** used only by `go test`; they are not compiled
into the importable `go-stemmers` library.

## `corpus.txt`

A word list derived from the public **OpenAlex** Works snapshot, which is released
under **CC0 1.0** (public domain dedication) — see https://openalex.org and
https://docs.openalex.org/. It was produced by `cmd/oacorpus`, which streams the
snapshot, tokenizes title/abstract/author/venue text into lowercased Unicode
letter/digit terms, and keeps the distinct vocabulary. Being CC0, it carries no
attribution or share-alike obligations.

The corpus is English-dominant with a long multilingual tail (accented Latin,
Cyrillic, Greek, Arabic, CJK, …). Coverage of any given script depends on what the
snapshot sample contains; some scripts (e.g. Tamil) may be absent, in which case
that language's golden simply exercises the stemmer's no-op path. The authoritative
per-language parity check is the live differential `TestRustgenParity`, which can
run over a larger streamed corpus.

## `res_<lang>.txt`

Each is `corpus.txt` stemmed by the corresponding algorithm, generated by the
`rustgen` oracle running **rust-stemmers 1.2.0** (../rustgen). They are the
byte-for-byte parity contract: `TestVectors` asserts the Go port reproduces them
exactly, and CI's oracle job regenerates them to confirm they still match
rust-stemmers.

## Regenerating

After bumping the pinned rust-stemmers version in ../rustgen/Cargo.toml:

    cargo build --release --manifest-path ../rustgen/Cargo.toml
    for pair in ar:arabic da:danish nl:dutch en:english fi:finnish fr:french \
                ger:german el:greek hu:hungarian it:italian no:norwegian \
                pt:portuguese ro:romanian ru:russian es:spanish sv:swedish \
                ta:tamil tr:turkish; do
      ../rustgen/target/release/rustgen "${pair#*:}" < corpus.txt > "res_${pair%%:*}.txt"
    done

To refresh `corpus.txt` itself, re-run `go run ./cmd/oacorpus -out testdata/corpus.txt`
and regenerate the goldens.

## Why this layout

Earlier revisions vendored the canonical Snowball / rust-stemmers test
vocabularies directly. Those carry mixed third-party licenses (BSD-3-Clause,
GPLv3 for the Arabic wordlist, CC BY-SA for several Wikipedia-derived ones), which
is undesirable in an MIT-licensed library even for test fixtures. Deriving the
input from CC0 OpenAlex and generating the expected output ourselves keeps the
whole corpus free of third-party licensing.
